lead.titleZh: 肩关节粘连性囊炎松动术效果，系统综述提供证据
lead.titleEn: Mobilization Techniques for Adhesive Capsulitis: SR Provides Evidence
lead.paragraphZh: 今天值得关注的头条来自一篇关于肩关节粘连性囊炎不同关节松动技术疗效的系统综述。在行业动态方面，APTA与CMS的政策变动也值得临床 PT 留意，它们预示着价值医疗的推进。
lead.paragraphEn: Today's headline features a systematic review comparing mobilization techniques for adhesive capsulitis. Industry updates from APTA and CMS policy shifts also warrant attention, signaling a move towards value-based care.

items.title: Comparative efficacy of different joint mobilization techniques in shoulder adhesive capsulitis: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
items.summary: A systematic review and meta-analysis compared different non-thrust joint mobilization techniques for shoulder adhesive capsulitis, finding generally similar clinical effects on objective and subjective outcomes.
items.curatedReason: 肩关节粘连性囊炎（冻结肩）非推拿关节松动术的系统综述和荟萃分析显示，不同技术间的疗效差异不大。在选择具体松动技术时，优先考虑你最熟练且患者依从性最高的方案。
items.titleZh: 肩关节粘连性囊炎不同关节松动技术的疗效比较：随机对照试验的系统综述和荟萃分析
items.summaryZh: 这项系统综述和荟萃分析比较了肩关节粘连性囊炎患者采用不同非推拿关节松动技术（单独或联合应用）对客观和主观结局的临床效果，发现总体疗效无显著差异。
items.curatedReasonEn: This systematic review and meta-analysis on non-thrust joint mobilization for adhesive capsulitis suggests no significant efficacy differences among various techniques. Prioritize techniques you are most proficient in and that maximize patient adherence.
items.limitation: 本综述检索窗口延长至 2025 年 11 月，但发表日期是 2026 年 7 月，可能存在部分纳入文献与检索时间点不完全一致的潜在风险，需警惕未来新证据。
items.limitationEn: The review's search window extending to November 2025 with a publication date of July 2026 suggests a potential risk of mismatch between included literature and search timeline, requiring vigilance for future evidence.
items.journal: The Journal of manual & manipulative therapy

items.title: The impact of anxiety/depression symptoms on return to work in patients with chronic neck or back pain: a longitudinal cohort study from the Norwegian neck and back registry | BMC Musculoskeletal Disorders
items.summary: A longitudinal cohort study from the Norwegian neck and back registry investigated the impact of anxiety/depression symptoms on return to work in patients with chronic neck or back pain, identifying them as leading causes of non-fatal health loss.
items.curatedReason: 焦虑/抑郁症状会显著影响慢性颈痛或腰痛患者的重返工作能力。这意味着，在康复方案中，你需要将患者的心理健康筛查和必要时的心理支持或转诊，纳入综合评估和管理。
items.titleZh: 焦虑/抑郁症状对慢性颈痛或腰痛患者重返工作的影响：一项挪威颈背部登记的纵向队列研究
items.summaryZh: 一项来自挪威颈背部登记的纵向队列研究调查了焦虑/抑郁症状对慢性颈痛或腰痛患者重返工作的影响，指出这些症状是非致命健康损失的主要原因。
items.curatedReasonEn: Anxiety/depression symptoms significantly impact the return-to-work ability of patients with chronic neck or back pain. This means you need to integrate mental health screening and, if necessary, psychological support or referral into your comprehensive assessment and management for these patients.
items.limitation: 作为队列研究，可能存在混杂因素影响重返工作结果，且其在挪威人群中的发现，外推至其他文化和医疗系统可能需要谨慎。
items.limitationEn: As a cohort study, confounding factors may influence return-to-work outcomes, and its findings in the Norwegian population may require caution when extrapolating to other cultures and healthcare systems.

items.title: Examination and treatment of the spine for upper extremity pain: a case series.
items.summary: This case series describes three patients with upper extremity pain who achieved clinically significant improvements in self-reported disability after receiving spine mobilization or manipulation techniques, even when their symptoms did not correspond with typical pain referral patterns.
items.curatedReason: 这份病例系列提示，当上肢疼痛呈现非典型模式时，不要只盯着局部，也要考虑评估和处理脊柱，特别是颈胸段脊柱功能障碍是否为潜在的疼痛来源。
items.titleZh: 脊柱检查与治疗对上肢疼痛的疗效：一个病例系列
items.summaryZh: 本病例系列报告了三名上肢疼痛患者，他们在接受脊柱松动或手法治疗后，自我报告残疾程度得到临床显著改善，即使症状不符合典型的疼痛放射模式。
items.curatedReasonEn: This case series suggests that when upper extremity pain presents atypically, consider evaluating and treating the spine, especially cervical and thoracic dysfunction, as a potential pain source, rather than focusing solely on local issues.
items.limitation: 本研究为病例系列，证据等级低，无法提供关于脊柱干预与上肢疼痛改善之间因果关系的强证据，需要高阶设计研究验证。
items.limitationEn: As a case series, this study provides low-level evidence and cannot establish a strong causal link between spinal intervention and upper extremity pain improvement; higher-level studies are needed for validation.
items.journal: The Journal of manual & manipulative therapy

items.title: Real-World Outcomes of a Telephone-Based Virtual Cognitive Rehabilitation Therapy Program: A Retrospective Cohort Analysis
items.summary: A retrospective observational analysis of 141 older adults with MCI or early-stage dementia showed a telephone-based virtual cognitive rehabilitation program combining SLP-led sessions with automated voice exercises improved daily functioning.
items.curatedReason: 对于患有轻度认知障碍或早期痴呆的患者，这种电话结合自动化语音的认知康复模式显示出实际疗效，证明了远程认知康复在提高可及性方面的潜力。
items.titleZh: 电话虚拟认知康复治疗项目的真实世界效果：一项回顾性队列分析
items.summaryZh: 一项对 141 名老年患者的回顾性观察分析显示，结合言语治疗师主导和自动化语音代理辅助的电话虚拟认知康复项目，能改善轻度认知障碍和早期痴呆患者的日常功能。
items.curatedReasonEn: For patients with mild cognitive impairment or early-stage dementia, this telephone-based cognitive rehabilitation model combining SLP-led sessions with automated voice exercises shows real-world efficacy, demonstrating the potential of remote cognitive rehabilitation to improve access.
items.limitation: 本研究为回顾性观察分析，可能存在选择偏倚和混杂因素，不能完全排除未知变量对结果的影响，且缺乏对照组比较。
items.limitationEn: This retrospective observational analysis may be subject to selection bias and confounding factors, as it cannot fully exclude the influence of unmeasured variables and lacks a control group for comparison.

items.title: Development and Preliminary Clinical Feasibility of a Wearable Nanovibration Delivery Device for Localised Bone Stimulation in Individuals with Spinal Cord Injury
items.summary: A wearable nanovibration delivery device was developed and demonstrated preliminary clinical feasibility for localized bone stimulation at the distal femur in individuals with spinal cord injury, aiming to address rapid bone loss in paralyzed lower limbs.
items.curatedReason: 这款可穿戴纳米振动设备有望作为脊髓损伤患者局部骨刺激的靶向干预手段。虽然目前仍处于初步可行性验证阶段，但它为未来预防和治疗 SCI 骨质疏松提供了新的研究方向。
items.titleZh: 可穿戴纳米振动递送设备在脊髓损伤患者局部骨刺激中的开发及初步临床可行性
items.summaryZh: 一项研究开发了一款可穿戴纳米振动递送设备，并在脊髓损伤患者的股骨远端局部骨刺激中展示了初步临床可行性，旨在解决瘫痪下肢的快速骨丢失问题。
items.curatedReasonEn: This wearable nanovibration device shows promise as a targeted intervention for localized bone stimulation in individuals with spinal cord injury. While currently in preliminary feasibility, it opens new research avenues for preventing and treating SCI-related osteoporosis.
items.limitation: 本研究为初步的临床可行性验证，样本量小，且尚未报告长期疗效和安全性数据。
items.limitationEn: This study is a preliminary clinical feasibility investigation with a small sample size and lacks long-term efficacy and safety data.

items.title: Task-Oriented training in a Patient with Triplegia: Post-Stroke Hemiparesis Complicating Pre-Existing Contralateral Upper Extremity Paralysis: A Case Report.
items.summary: A case report of a 75-year-old man with triplegia following stroke, demonstrating that intensive inpatient task-oriented training led to significant improvements in Fugl-Meyer Assessment-UE scores (from 3 to 43 by 23 weeks) and functional independence, maintained at 1-year follow-up.
items.curatedReason: 这个病例报告展示了针对三肢瘫患者的任务导向训练在卒中后早期干预中的潜力。虽然只是个案，但也提示了复杂病例的功能恢复路径。
items.titleZh: 三肢瘫患者的任务导向训练：一例既往对侧上肢瘫痪后并发卒中偏瘫的病例报告。
items.summaryZh: 一例 75 岁卒中后三肢瘫患者的病例报告显示，强化住院任务导向训练使其 Fugl-Meyer 评估上肢评分从 3 分提高到 23 周时的 43 分，功能独立性显著改善，并在 1 年随访时得以维持。
items.curatedReasonEn: This case report illustrates the potential of task-oriented training for triplegia patients in early post-stroke intervention. While a single case, it offers insights into functional recovery pathways for complex cases.
items.limitation: 单病例报告，结果无法泛化。
items.limitationEn: Single case report, results are not generalizable.
items.journal: American journal of physical medicine & rehabilitation

items.title: Retrospective analysis of the relationship between post-stroke fatigue and aphasia severity
items.summary: A retrospective analysis explored the relationship between post-stroke fatigue, a common and chronic condition affecting 25-85% of stroke survivors, and aphasia severity. It emphasizes the variability in reported prevalence due to methodological differences and the long-term impact of fatigue on quality of life.
items.curatedReason: 本回顾性分析强调了卒中后疲劳与失语症严重程度之间的潜在关联。理解这种关系能帮助你更全面地评估卒中患者，特别是在制定康复计划时，应将疲劳作为关键因素纳入考量。
items.titleZh: 卒中后疲劳与失语症严重程度之间关系的回顾性分析。
items.summaryZh: 一项回顾性分析探讨了卒中后疲劳（一种影响 25-85% 卒中幸存者的常见慢性疾病）与失语症严重程度之间的关系。该研究强调了由于方法学差异导致的患病率报告变异性，以及疲劳对生活质量的长期影响。
items.curatedReasonEn: This retrospective analysis highlights a potential association between post-stroke fatigue and aphasia severity. Understanding this relationship can help you conduct more comprehensive assessments of stroke patients, especially when developing rehabilitation plans, where fatigue should be considered a key factor.
items.limitation: 回顾性研究，存在潜在的选择偏差和混杂因素。
items.limitationEn: Retrospective study with potential selection bias and confounding factors.
items.journal: Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation

items.title: APTA, Hartford HealthCare Launch Frailty Pilot to Advance Value-Based Care
items.summary: APTA and Hartford HealthCare have launched a pilot program focusing on frailty to advance value-based care, highlighting the expanding role of physical therapy in preventive and integrated health models.
items.curatedReason: APTA 此次联手 Hartford HealthCare 开展衰弱试点项目，明确了一个信号：物理治疗在价值医疗体系中的地位正日益巩固。留意你所在区域是否有这类预示 PT 市场机会的整合性项目，尤其是针对老年衰弱的预防和管理。
items.titleZh: APTA 与 Hartford HealthCare 启动衰弱试点项目，推进价值医疗
items.summaryZh: APTA 与 Hartford HealthCare 合作启动一项衰弱试点项目，旨在推进价值医疗，突显了物理治疗在预防和整合健康模型中日益增长的作用。
items.curatedReasonEn: This APTA-Hartford HealthCare frailty pilot solidifies physical therapy's growing role within value-based care models. Keep an eye out for similar integrated programs in your market, especially those targeting geriatric frailty prevention and management, as they signal direct PT opportunities.

items.title: Read more about CMS Proposes Transformational Medicare Reforms to Expand Accountable Care, Modernize Physician Payment, and Shift from Sick Care to Healthcare
items.summary: CMS proposes transformational Medicare reforms to expand accountable care, modernize physician payment, and shift from sick care to healthcare.
items.curatedReason: CMS 正在提议进行重大医保改革，旨在扩大责任制医疗（Accountable Care）并实现医生支付现代化，将重心从病患治疗转向健康维护。这些改革有望影响你未来与医保体系的互动方式，包括报销结构和提供服务的模式。
items.titleZh: CMS 提议改革医保，旨在扩大责任制医疗、实现医生支付现代化，并将重心从疾病治疗转向健康维护
items.summaryZh: 美国医疗保险和医疗补助服务中心（CMS）提议对医保进行变革性改革，以扩大责任制医疗、实现医生支付现代化，并将服务重心从疾病治疗转向健康维护。
items.curatedReasonEn: CMS is proposing significant Medicare reforms aimed at expanding Accountable Care and modernizing physician payment, shifting the focus from sick care to healthcare. These reforms are likely to impact how you interact with the Medicare system in the future, including reimbursement structures and service delivery models.

items.title: Read more about Calendar Year (CY) 2027 Medicare Physician Fee Schedule Proposed Rule (CMS-1848-P) — Medicare Shared Savings Program Proposals
items.summary: The Calendar Year (CY) 2027 Medicare Physician Fee Schedule Proposed Rule (CMS-1848-P) includes proposals related to the Medicare Shared Savings Program.
items.curatedReason: CMS 2027 医保医生收费标准拟议规则（Physician Fee Schedule Proposed Rule）包含针对 Medicare Shared Savings Program (MSSP) 的重要调整。这些提议直接影响你在美国作为物理治疗师的收入和业务模式，特别是如果你参与了 ACO（Accountable Care Organization）模式。仔细审阅这些细节，它们将塑造未来你的执业环境。
items.titleZh: 2027 日历年医保医生收费标准拟议规则（CMS-1848-P）—— Medicare 共享储蓄计划提案
items.summaryZh: 2027 日历年医保医生收费标准拟议规则（CMS-1848-P）提出了关于 Medicare 共享储蓄计划（Medicare Shared Savings Program）的建议。
items.curatedReasonEn: The CMS Calendar Year (CY) 2027 Medicare Physician Fee Schedule Proposed Rule includes significant adjustments to the Medicare Shared Savings Program (MSSP). These proposals directly impact your earnings and business model as a physical therapist in the U.S., especially if you participate in Accountable Care Organizations (ACOs). Review these details carefully, as they will shape your future practice environment.

items.title: WebPT Introduces the Next Generation of AI-Powered Revenue Cycle Management for Rehab Therapy
items.summary: WebPT has introduced its next generation of AI-powered Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) solutions for rehab therapy, aiming to optimize billing and payment processes for clinics.
items.curatedReason: WebPT 的新一代 AI-RCM 解决方案，旨在优化计费和支付流程。这意味着你的诊所若能有效整合，有望在索赔管理和支付效率上实现显著提升。
items.titleZh: WebPT 推出新一代 AI 驱动的康复治疗营收周期管理解决方案
items.summaryZh: WebPT 发布了新一代 AI 驱动的康复治疗营收周期管理（RCM）解决方案，旨在优化诊所的计费和支付流程。
items.curatedReasonEn: WebPT's new AI-RCM solution aims to optimize billing and payment processes. This means your clinic could see significant gains in claims management and payment efficiency if integrated effectively.
